note: Canonical People Analytics metric registry (ISO 30414-aligned). One definition per metric, so the numbers an agent reports are the numbers everyone trusts. Agents READ and CITE this file; they never redefine metrics. metric_class separates Core KPIs from Diagnostics and Operational Alerts; protocol carries the implementation rule; aggregate_formula gives the correct group/portfolio form where it differs from the per-unit formula. agent_forbidden_actions make this a measurement-GOVERNANCE system.

metrics.name: Open requisitions
metrics.definition: Count of approved, active requisitions at the as-of date (open-approved only, not drafts).
metrics.formula: count(reqs where status = 'open' and approved = true and on_hold = false)
metrics.exclusions: drafts / pending-approval reqs; on-hold, filled, and closed reqs
metrics.common_misuse: Treating open reqs as demand without netting against approved headcount, or counting drafts as demand.
metrics.decision_supported: Where to focus sourcing capacity this week.
metrics.protocol: Point-in-time count of distinct req_ids; report open-approved as the headline, drafts separately.
metrics.aggregate_formula: openings_remaining = sum(openings_approved - openings_filled) when a req carries multiple openings

metrics.name: Time to fill
metrics.definition: Calendar days from requisition APPROVED to offer accepted (recruiter-controllable clock).
metrics.exclusions: cancelled reqs; clock pauses during on-hold intervals
metrics.common_misuse: Reporting a lone mean on a right-skewed distribution; starting the clock at req-created so HM/finance approval latency is charged to recruiters; dropping never-filled reqs (survivorship) so the hard searches are invisible.
metrics.decision_supported: Capacity planning and hiring-plan commit dates.
metrics.protocol: Report p50 AND p90 (never a lone mean); clock starts at req-approved; segment by job family/level/geo; pair with open-req aging so the unfilled tail stays visible.
metrics.aggregate_formula: report p50 and p90 by segment; track 'approval latency' (created->approved) and 'time to start' (req_approved->start_date) as companions

metrics.name: Time to hire
metrics.definition: Calendar days from a candidate's first touch to offer accepted (candidate-experience clock).
metrics.exclusions: non-hired candidates (only resolvable for accepted candidates)
metrics.common_misuse: Confusing time-to-hire (candidate clock) with time-to-fill (req clock); excluding sourced candidates by keying only on application_date, which hides the fastest passive-talent hires.
metrics.protocol: first_touch = min(application_date, first_recruiter_contact_date); report p50 and p90 over hired candidates.

metrics.name: Requisition aging
metrics.definition: A requisition is 'aging' when it has been open (from approved) longer than a self-calibrating threshold; the example agent uses a flat fallback.
metrics.formula: days_open(from approved) > k * median_time_to_fill(job_family, level, geo)   (flat fallback: days_open > aging_threshold_days)
metrics.exclusions: on-hold reqs (clock pauses during on-hold)
metrics.common_misuse: Using one flat threshold across markets with very different natural fill times.
metrics.decision_supported: Which searches need intervention or re-scoping.
metrics.protocol: Self-calibrating k ~= 1.5 x segment median TTF is preferred; flat aging_threshold_days is a documented fallback the ta-reporting example uses.

metrics.name: Requisition staleness
metrics.definition: A requisition is 'stale' when it has had no SUBSTANTIVE human pipeline activity for longer than the stale threshold.
metrics.exclusions: on-hold reqs; automated/system status writes do not count as updates
metrics.common_misuse: Counting automated status pings or candidate self-updates as recruiter 'updates'.
metrics.protocol: Substantive update = candidate added, stage change, interview scheduled, or recruiter note; exclude automated syncs.

metrics.name: Thin pipeline
metrics.definition: A high-priority (P1) requisition whose stage-weighted expected hires fall below a safety factor; the example agent uses a flat active-candidate count.
metrics.formula: priority = 'P1' AND expected_hires < safety_factor   where expected_hires = sum_stage(count_at_stage * passthrough(stage->hire))   (flat fallback: pipeline_depth < thin_threshold)
metrics.exclusions: non-P1 reqs (triage scope, not a measurement limit)
metrics.common_misuse: Treating raw pipeline count as quality without stage weighting (3 at 'applied' != 3 at 'onsite').
metrics.decision_supported: Where to add sourcing before a search stalls.
metrics.protocol: Stage-weighted expected-yield is preferred; flat thin_threshold on active (post-screen, not dispositioned) candidates is the ta-reporting fallback.

metrics.name: Offer acceptance rate
metrics.definition: Share of resolved written offers that were accepted, over a closed offer cohort.
metrics.exclusions: company-rescinded offers; offers still pending (not counted as declines)
metrics.common_misuse: Counting unresolved-pending offers as declines, or mismatching numerator/denominator across period boundaries; reading a low rate as comp-only.
metrics.protocol: Closed cohort: offers extended in the window, measured once each reaches a final disposition; 'extended' = written offer.

metrics.name: Stage pass-through rate
metrics.definition: Share of candidates advancing from one funnel stage to the next, over a matured stage cohort.
metrics.exclusions: candidates still in stage (no final outcome yet)
metrics.common_misuse: Comparing pass-through across roles with different bars; reporting stage-local rates without the cumulative funnel yield.
metrics.protocol: Mature stage-entry cohort; handle re-entry/skips/same-day transitions explicitly.
metrics.aggregate_formula: cumulative funnel yield = hires / top_of_funnel_candidates (app -> offer -> hire)

metrics.name: Quality of hire
metrics.definition: Composite, indexed 0-100, of post-hire success: early performance, first-year retention, hiring-manager satisfaction, and time to productivity.
metrics.exclusions: cohorts too recent to observe the component signals
metrics.common_misuse: Optimizing for speed/cost while quality silently drops; using an unweighted average of incomparable sub-scores.
metrics.decision_supported: Whether faster/cheaper hiring is still good hiring.
metrics.protocol: Composite indexed 0-100: each component is normalized 0-100 then combined with the params.weights (sum=1.0); publish the weights; score only matured cohorts.

metrics.name: Cost per hire
metrics.definition: Fully loaded recruiting cost per hire (ANSI/SHRM-2012 standard).
metrics.exclusions: non-recruiting costs; relocation/sign-on (report separately)
metrics.common_misuse: Counting only external (agency) spend and omitting internal cost, or vice-versa.
metrics.protocol: Use the ANSI/SHRM-2012 definition verbatim; state the period and the internal-cost components included.

metrics.name: Source-channel effectiveness
metrics.definition: Yield (and downstream quality) of each recruiting source.
metrics.exclusions: untracked / referral-of-referral attribution gaps
metrics.common_misuse: Ranking channels on volume alone without quality-of-hire or cost per channel.
metrics.protocol: Pair yield with quality_of_hire and cost per channel; state the attribution model (first-touch vs last-touch).
metrics.aggregate_formula: pair yield with quality_of_hire_by_source = mean(quality_of_hire | source) and cost_per_hire_by_source; rank channels on quality-adjusted yield, not raw yield

metrics.name: Recruiter capacity
metrics.definition: Active open-approved requisitions carried per recruiter.
metrics.exclusions: reqs on hold; non-requisition work
metrics.common_misuse: Reading a single load number without role-mix difficulty (a senior eng search != a high-volume role).
metrics.protocol: Report distribution, not just the mean; weight by role difficulty where possible.

metrics.name: Headcount
metrics.definition: Active worker count at the as-of date (point-in-time), including employees on protected leave.
metrics.formula: count(workers where worker_type = 'employee' and status in active_like_statuses)
metrics.exclusions: contractors (counted separately); pending-starts (until start date); terminated workers
metrics.common_misuse: Mixing point-in-time headcount with average headcount when annualizing; excluding employees on protected leave.
metrics.protocol: Active_like includes protected leave (LOA/parental/disability); state treatment of interns/fixed-term.

metrics.name: FTE
metrics.definition: Full-time-equivalent count, weighting each assignment by scheduled hours against the region's full-time standard.
metrics.exclusions: contractors
metrics.common_misuse: Reporting FTE as headcount in finance reconciliations; using one global full-time-hours constant across regions.
metrics.protocol: standard_full_time_hours is region-aware; cap individual FTE at 1.0 for headcount-equivalent (uncapped only for cost/capacity with OT).

metrics.name: Net headcount growth
metrics.definition: Change in active headcount over a period, defined as ending minus beginning (reconciles exactly).
metrics.exclusions: n/a (the bridge accounts for every component)
metrics.common_misuse: Reporting hires - exits as the headline: it won't tie to the roster because it misses conversions, rehires, returns from leave, and net transfers.
metrics.protocol: Headline is ending - beginning; publish the bridge = hires + conversions + rehires + returns - voluntary - involuntary +/- net_transfers.
metrics.aggregate_formula: bridge: ending = beginning + hires + conversions + rehires + returns_from_leave - voluntary_exits - involuntary_exits +/- net_transfers

metrics.name: Span of control
metrics.definition: Average number of direct reports per people-manager (counts every manager, including managers of managers).
metrics.exclusions: dotted-line reports
metrics.common_misuse: Using count(ICs)/count(managers), which drops managers who report to managers and makes the org look flatter than it is; reporting the mean alone.
metrics.protocol: Equivalently, the mean of each manager's direct-report count; report median and the distribution, not a lone mean.
metrics.aggregate_formula: report mean AND median span, plus the full distribution

metrics.name: Span outlier rate
metrics.definition: Share of people-managers whose span falls outside the target band (sub-scale or overloaded).
metrics.formula: count(managers with span < low_band OR span > high_band) / count(people_managers)
metrics.exclusions: non-people-managers
metrics.common_misuse: Watching only the average span; the outliers (sub-scale managers) are what actually drive delayering.
metrics.protocol: Report sub-scale (< low_band) and overloaded (> high_band) separately; sub-scale managers are the delayering signal.

metrics.name: Management layers
metrics.definition: Reporting depth from the top executive to individual contributors, reported as a distribution (not just the deepest chain).
metrics.exclusions: vacant manager nodes
metrics.common_misuse: Reporting max(depth) alone -- one outlier chain is a 'tallest point', not an org-health read.
metrics.protocol: State the CEO index convention (CEO = layer 0 or 1); keep max as one stat but headline the layer distribution + median depth.
metrics.aggregate_formula: employees per depth level (layer distribution) + median depth-to-IC; max(depth) as a secondary stat

metrics.name: Vacancy rate
metrics.definition: Approved open positions as a share of total budgeted establishment.
metrics.exclusions: unapproved/pipeline positions
metrics.common_misuse: Counting reqs without an approved position behind them; using just-vacant rather than total establishment as the denominator.
metrics.protocol: Denominator = budgeted establishment (filled + approved-open positions), not req counts; optionally time-weight over the period.

metrics.name: Contingent-workforce ratio
metrics.definition: Contractors as a share of the blended workforce.
metrics.exclusions: n/a
metrics.common_misuse: Excluding contractors from every other metric without one measure that tracks the blended workforce shape.
metrics.protocol: Define 'contractor' (SOW vs staff-aug vs 1099) consistently with how the HRIS classifies worker_type.

metrics.name: Headcount plan attainment
metrics.definition: Actual headcount against the approved plan.
metrics.exclusions: n/a
metrics.common_misuse: Comparing actual to a stale plan version; mixing FTE-based plans with people-based actuals.
metrics.protocol: Pin the plan version and the basis (FTE vs people); state the as-of date.

metrics.name: Compa-ratio
metrics.definition: Base salary divided by the midpoint of the salary range for the employee's job profile, level, and location.
metrics.exclusions: employees with no mapped band; variable/commission pay
metrics.common_misuse: Comparing across roles/geographies without controlling for range structure; reporting mean-of-ratios as the group figure; assuming midpoint = market when midpoints lag.
metrics.protocol: FTE-normalize part-time base before the ratio; for groups use the aggregate form, not the mean of individual ratios; pair with a market ratio when midpoints may be stale.
metrics.aggregate_formula: group compa-ratio = sum(base) / sum(midpoint)  (NOT mean(base_i/midpoint_i)); market ratio = base / market_p50

metrics.name: Range penetration
metrics.definition: Position within the band: where base salary sits between range min and max.
metrics.exclusions: unbanded roles; degenerate (zero-width) ranges
metrics.common_misuse: Using penetration where compa-ratio (midpoint comparison) was intended; reporting mean-of-ratios for groups.
metrics.decision_supported: Where employees sit in band; merit headroom.
metrics.protocol: With a symmetric range, compa-ratio 1.0 == 50% penetration; they diverge with asymmetric ranges or varying spreads. Use the aggregate form for group views.
metrics.aggregate_formula: group penetration = sum(base - min) / sum(max - min)

metrics.name: Out-of-band rate
metrics.definition: Share of banded employees whose base salary falls outside their band, split below-min vs above-max.
metrics.formula: below_min_rate = count(base < range_min)/count(banded);  above_max_rate = count(base > range_max)/count(banded);  combined = below + above
metrics.exclusions: unbanded roles; red-circled with a documented exception (reported as a sub-population)
metrics.common_misuse: Reporting one combined rate that masks direction (below-min = compression/equity; above-max = red-circle/promo/band design); flagging above-max without checking for approved exceptions.
metrics.protocol: Report below_min_rate and above_max_rate separately, plus combined; boundary inclusive (base == min/max is in band); track red-circled-with-exception as a reported sub-population (needed for true-up budget).

metrics.name: Merit increase %
metrics.definition: Percentage base-pay increase awarded in the merit cycle; headline is the dollar-weighted spend, not the average of percents.
metrics.exclusions: promotions and market adjustments (separate events); sub-threshold new hires and ineligible/LOA per policy
metrics.common_misuse: Blending merit with promotion increases; reporting the average of individual percents (mis-weights by salary mix) as the budget figure.
metrics.protocol: Separate three numbers people conflate: budget (planned), spend (actual), and average increase per recipient; define the eligible population.

metrics.name: Compensation exception rate
metrics.definition: Share of guideline-eligible comp actions that REQUIRED an out-of-guideline exception (approved + denied), by action type.
metrics.exclusions: auto-approved within-guideline actions; actions not subject to guideline review
metrics.common_misuse: Counting approved-only (which conflates request volume with approval leniency); using one denominator across offer/merit/promo/adjustment so the band misfit is hidden.
metrics.protocol: Numerator = exceptions requested (approved + denied); report separately for offers, merit, promo, market adjustments.

metrics.name: Benefits enrollment rate
metrics.definition: Take-up: share of eligible employees enrolled in a given benefit.
metrics.exclusions: ineligible populations; employees still inside a waiting period
metrics.common_misuse: Using enrolled/total-employees instead of enrolled/eligible; treating auto-enroll defaults as active engagement.
metrics.protocol: Distinguish active elections from auto-enroll defaults; pair with waiver_rate; for 401(k) report participation + average deferral %.

metrics.name: Adjusted pay gap (pay equity)
metrics.definition: Regression-adjusted residual pay difference between demographic groups after controlling for legitimate factors.
metrics.formula: exp(coef_on_demographic in regression of ln(pay) ~ demographic + level + job_family + location + tenure) - 1
metrics.exclusions: Groups with fewer than 10 members on either side of a contrast (estimate suppressed, not zero); performance rating (excluded as a potentially biased control).
metrics.common_misuse: Reading raw/unadjusted gaps as discrimination, or treating compa-ratio as a pay-equity metric; omitting legitimate controls or over-controlling with tainted variables.
metrics.protocol: Report alongside the raw gap; document the controls; never expose an individual's residual. Performance rating is deliberately NOT a control (it can carry the same bias being measured - over-controlling masks discrimination). A minimum focal-cell size (>=10 per side) is enforced; smaller groups are suppressed, not reported.

metrics.name: Raw (unadjusted) pay gap
metrics.definition: Unadjusted median pay difference between two demographic groups.
metrics.exclusions: groups below the minimum reportable size
metrics.common_misuse: Presenting the raw gap as evidence of discrimination without the adjusted analysis, or hiding it because it looks worse than adjusted.
metrics.decision_supported: Transparency context for the adjusted analysis.
metrics.protocol: Always report with the adjusted_pay_gap; state the pay measure (base vs total cash).

metrics.name: CEO pay ratio
metrics.definition: Ratio of CEO total compensation to the median employee's annual total compensation.
metrics.exclusions: the CEO from the median calculation; per SEC methodology
metrics.common_misuse: Comparing the ratio across companies with different workforce mixes as if it were a like-for-like figure.
metrics.decision_supported: Required US public-company disclosure (Dodd-Frank 953(b)) and governance.
metrics.protocol: Follow the SEC pay-ratio methodology for identifying the median employee; annual cadence.

metrics.name: Total cash compensation (TCC)
metrics.definition: Base salary plus target (or actual) cash variable.
metrics.exclusions: equity/LTI (see total direct comp); benefits
metrics.common_misuse: Benchmarking competitiveness on base alone, which understates rewards in bonus-heavy roles.
metrics.protocol: State target vs actual; FTE-normalize; benchmark at TCC, not base.
metrics.aggregate_formula: TCC positioning = TCC / market_reference_point (e.g. market p50)

metrics.name: Bonus / target attainment
metrics.definition: Actual variable pay relative to target, and pool funding.
metrics.exclusions: employees not on a bonus plan; prorated new hires (handle explicitly)
metrics.common_misuse: Reading attainment without pool funding %, or blending plans with different target structures.
metrics.protocol: Report alongside pool_funding% = funded_pool / target_pool; segment by plan.

metrics.name: Labor cost per FTE
metrics.definition: Total workforce labor cost per FTE.
metrics.exclusions: non-labor opex
metrics.common_misuse: Mixing fully-loaded cost (with benefits/taxes) and base-only cost across reports; using headcount instead of average FTE.
metrics.protocol: State what 'labor cost' includes (base, variable, benefits, employer taxes); also report as % of revenue/opex.

metrics.name: Benefits cost per employee
metrics.definition: Employer benefits spend per employee.
metrics.exclusions: employee-paid contributions
metrics.common_misuse: Comparing spend without enrollment/utilization context; mixing per-employee and per-enrolled denominators.
metrics.protocol: State whether the denominator is all employees or the enrolled population; pair with enrollment + utilization.

metrics.name: Voluntary attrition
metrics.definition: Annualized rate of employee-initiated departures.
metrics.exclusions: involuntary exits (RIF, for-cause); end-of-contract; internal transfers; deaths (conventionally excluded)
metrics.common_misuse: Using point-in-time instead of average headcount; leaving the annualization method unstated; compounding instead of simple scaling.
metrics.protocol: SIMPLE annualization x(12/months), NO compounding; average_headcount = mean of monthly actives; voluntary = employee-initiated only.

metrics.name: Regrettable attrition
metrics.definition: Annualized rate of voluntary departures the company would have preferred to keep (criteria-based, not manager discretion).
metrics.exclusions: non-regrettable and involuntary exits
metrics.common_misuse: Inconsistent 'regrettable' tagging across managers makes trends unreliable; leaving annualization unstated.
metrics.protocol: Replace manager discretion with a calibrated rule (performance >= threshold and/or critical role and/or high potential); same simple annualization.

metrics.name: Total turnover rate
metrics.definition: Annualized rate of all separations (voluntary + involuntary). The ISO 30414 headline turnover figure.
metrics.exclusions: internal transfers
metrics.common_misuse: Reporting voluntary attrition without total turnover, so voluntary can't be contextualized.
metrics.protocol: Same simple annualization and average-headcount denominator as voluntary; voluntary and involuntary are sub-cuts of this.

metrics.name: Involuntary turnover rate
metrics.definition: Annualized rate of company-initiated separations (RIF, for-cause).
metrics.exclusions: voluntary exits; end-of-contract
metrics.common_misuse: Folding involuntary into a single turnover number so workforce actions are invisible.
metrics.protocol: Same annualization; keep distinct from voluntary so total = voluntary + involuntary.

metrics.name: New-hire attrition
metrics.definition: Share of a MATURED hire cohort who left within their first 12 months.
metrics.exclusions: internal transfers; cohorts hired < 12 months ago (not yet matured)
metrics.common_misuse: Including immature cohorts (hired < 12 months ago) which mechanically understates; mixing voluntary and involuntary first-year exits.
metrics.protocol: Restrict to cohorts hired >= 12 months ago (or use survival analysis); split voluntary vs involuntary.

metrics.name: Early attrition (90-day)
metrics.definition: Share of a matured hire cohort who left within 90 days -- a mis-sell/onboarding leading indicator.
metrics.exclusions: cohorts hired < 90 days ago
metrics.common_misuse: Treating a 90-day exit the same as a 12-month exit -- the causes (mis-sell, bad onboarding) differ.
metrics.protocol: Companion to new_hire_attrition; matured (>= 90 days) cohorts only; split voluntary vs involuntary.

metrics.name: 12-month retention
metrics.definition: Share of a matured hire cohort still employed 12 months later.
metrics.exclusions: cohorts not yet 12 months matured
metrics.common_misuse: Assuming retention and attrition sum to 100% across different denominators or exit definitions.
metrics.protocol: Clean complement of new_hire_attrition only under identical denominators + exit definitions; transfers count as retained at company level.

metrics.name: Internal mobility rate
metrics.definition: Share of EMPLOYEES who made at least one qualifying internal role change in the period (person-rate).
metrics.formula: count(distinct employees with >= 1 qualifying internal move) / average_headcount
metrics.exclusions: title-only changes with no role change; reorg re-mappings
metrics.common_misuse: Counting moves instead of distinct movers (one person moving twice double-counts); counting reorg re-mappings as mobility.
metrics.protocol: Person-rate: distinct movers / average headcount. Publish the event-rate and the TA-side internal_fill_rate separately.
metrics.aggregate_formula: event-rate = internal_moves / average_headcount (report separately, clearly labeled)

metrics.name: Internal fill rate
metrics.definition: Share of filled vacancies filled by an internal candidate (the build-vs-buy view).
metrics.exclusions: contractor conversions (state treatment)
metrics.common_misuse: Conflating the employee-experience mobility rate with the TA build-vs-buy fill rate.
metrics.protocol: Distinct from internal_mobility_rate (which is person-based); this is vacancy-based.

metrics.name: Promotion rate
metrics.definition: Share of the eligible population promoted in the period, cut by level.
metrics.formula: count(distinct promoted_employees in level) / promotion_eligible_population in level
metrics.exclusions: lateral moves; top-of-track and sub-tenure (excluded from the eligible denominator)
metrics.common_misuse: Reporting one blended rate across levels (P2->P3 common, Dir->VP rare) so the number is near-meaningless; ignoring the level pyramid.
metrics.protocol: Always cut by level/band; use a promotion-eligible denominator; enable demographic cuts for advancement equity.
metrics.aggregate_formula: enterprise rate = distinct promoted / average_headcount; fairness rate = distinct promoted / average_eligible_population

metrics.name: Promotion velocity (time in level)
metrics.definition: Median time employees spend in a level before promotion.
metrics.exclusions: employees who haven't been promoted (use survival methods if needed)
metrics.common_misuse: Reporting a mean on a right-skewed duration; comparing across levels with different natural tenures.
metrics.protocol: Report median (and p90); cut by level; enable demographic cuts to surface advancement-equity gaps.

metrics.name: Performance rating distribution
metrics.definition: Distribution of performance ratings across the rated population for a cycle.
metrics.exclusions: unrated (new hires, leave) -- reported as a companion share, not silently dropped
metrics.common_misuse: Forcing a distribution and reading the shape as performance truth; hiding the unrated share so the cycle looks healthier.
metrics.protocol: Report unrated_share = unrated / total_in_scope alongside; the value is in the cuts (by manager, level, demographic).

metrics.name: Case volume
metrics.definition: Count of distinct People Ops cases opened in the period, also reported per capita.
metrics.exclusions: spam/duplicate cases; inbound messages (count cases, not messages)
metrics.common_misuse: Reading raw volume as failure rather than adoption; not normalizing per capita as headcount changes.
metrics.protocol: Count distinct case_id, not messages; segment by category (the mix matters more than the total).

metrics.name: SLA attainment
metrics.definition: Share of SLA-due cases resolved within their SLA target, counting open-but-breached cases against the rate.
metrics.exclusions: cases without an SLA target
metrics.common_misuse: Using within_sla/resolved only -- it ignores open cases that already breached, so it overstates and is gameable (leave breached cases open).
metrics.protocol: Denominator includes open-and-breached; or use a due-based cohort evaluated at resolution or breach (whichever first); pair with reopen_rate.

metrics.name: Time to resolution
metrics.definition: Wall-clock elapsed time from case open to resolved.
metrics.exclusions: n/a (state reopened-time treatment explicitly)
metrics.common_misuse: Reporting a lone mean on skewed data; using wall-clock that penalizes after-hours/weekend cases.
metrics.decision_supported: Where to automate or add staff.
metrics.protocol: Report median + p90 on WALL-CLOCK elapsed time (the reference engine computes resolved_at - opened_at); a production deployment refines this to an SLA/business-hours calendar. Segment by type/priority; pick one canonical reopen treatment (full lifecycle = truth).

metrics.name: Self-service deflection
metrics.definition: Share of inquiries genuinely resolved via self-service (with a resolution signal), excluding those that became human cases.
metrics.exclusions: sessions that bypass tracked channels; sessions followed by a human case (no double-count)
metrics.common_misuse: Counting a KB view with no follow-up as 'resolved' -- the person may have abandoned or gone to Slack; inflating via channel leakage.
metrics.protocol: Require a resolution signal (explicit 'this helped' OR no follow-up case from the same user on the same topic within N days); state channel-coverage limits.

metrics.name: Reopen rate
metrics.definition: Share of resolved cases that were reopened -- the guard against SLA gaming and premature closure.
metrics.exclusions: cases reopened for unrelated new issues (state the window)
metrics.common_misuse: Hitting SLA by closing cases prematurely; this metric is the counterweight that exposes it.
metrics.decision_supported: Quality check on SLA attainment and resolution.
metrics.protocol: Define the reopen window (e.g. reopened within N days of resolution); pair with sla_attainment and time_to_resolution.

metrics.name: First-contact resolution (FCR)
metrics.definition: Share of resolved cases closed on the first touch with no follow-up.
metrics.exclusions: cases requiring inherently multi-step handling (segment separately)
metrics.common_misuse: Driving FCR so hard that agents close-and-reopen; read it with reopen_rate.
metrics.protocol: Read alongside reopen_rate so 'first-touch closes' aren't gamed via reopens.

metrics.name: Case CSAT
metrics.definition: Satisfaction with resolved People Ops cases.
metrics.exclusions: cases with no survey response (note response-rate bias)
metrics.common_misuse: Reading an SLA hit as a satisfied employee; ignoring low survey response rates.
metrics.decision_supported: Employee experience of HR service, beyond speed.
metrics.protocol: Report response rate alongside; an SLA hit != a satisfied employee.

metrics.name: Open-case backlog / aging
metrics.definition: Open cases bucketed by age, with breached cases highlighted.
metrics.formula: count(open cases) by age_bucket; breached = open and past SLA
metrics.exclusions: resolved/closed cases
metrics.common_misuse: Watching only inflow/throughput while an aging breached backlog grows unseen.
metrics.decision_supported: Where the queue is silting up; feeds the SLA-attainment denominator.
metrics.protocol: This open-and-breached population is exactly what sla_attainment's corrected denominator needs.

metrics.name: Recordable incident rate (TRIR)
metrics.definition: OSHA-style total recordable incident rate per 100 FTE-years.
metrics.exclusions: first-aid-only events below the recordable threshold
metrics.common_misuse: Comparing TRIR across industries with different baseline risk; using headcount instead of hours worked.
metrics.protocol: 200,000 = 100 FTE x 2,000 hours; use actual hours worked as the base.

metrics.name: Lost-time injury rate (LTIFR)
metrics.definition: Lost-time injuries per 1,000,000 hours worked (LTIFR, ILO convention).
metrics.exclusions: recordable injuries with no lost time
metrics.common_misuse: Conflating TRIR (all recordables) with LTIFR (lost-time only).
metrics.protocol: Per 1,000,000 hours worked (LTIFR). Distinct from the OSHA recordable rate (TRIR), which is per 200,000 hours — keep the two bases separate and consistent across periods.

metrics.name: Absence rate
metrics.definition: Share of scheduled work time lost to unplanned absence.
metrics.exclusions: approved planned leave (PTO/vacation); company holidays
metrics.common_misuse: Folding planned PTO into 'absence'; not normalizing by scheduled (not calendar) days.
metrics.protocol: Unplanned absence only; denominator = scheduled workdays; segment by org unit.

metrics.name: Grievance rate
metrics.definition: Formal employee grievances per 100 FTE in the period.
metrics.exclusions: informal concerns not filed as formal grievances
metrics.common_misuse: Reading a rising rate as deteriorating culture vs rising trust in the process; comparing across very different reporting cultures.
metrics.decision_supported: ER risk and culture signal (ISO 30414 compliance & ethics).
metrics.protocol: Per 100 FTE; segment by type; pair with resolution time and substantiation rate.

metrics.name: Disciplinary action rate
metrics.definition: Formal disciplinary actions per 100 FTE in the period.
metrics.exclusions: informal coaching/feedback
metrics.common_misuse: Treating volume alone as a culture verdict without severity or substantiation context.
metrics.protocol: Per 100 FTE; segment by severity and outcome; watch for inconsistency across managers/groups.

metrics.name: Ethics-hotline cases
metrics.definition: Ethics/whistleblower reports per 100 FTE, with substantiation rate.
metrics.exclusions: spam/non-actionable contacts
metrics.common_misuse: Reading low report volume as a healthy culture (it often signals low trust in the channel).
metrics.protocol: Per 100 FTE; report substantiation rate and time-to-close; protect reporter anonymity (never identify).

metrics.name: Training hours per FTE
metrics.definition: Average formal learning hours per FTE in the period.
metrics.exclusions: untracked informal learning
metrics.common_misuse: Treating hours as outcomes; volume of training != capability gained.
metrics.protocol: Per FTE; pair with completion and capability/skill outcomes, not hours alone.

metrics.name: Training completion rate
metrics.definition: Share of assigned learning that was completed.
metrics.exclusions: optional/self-directed content not assigned
metrics.common_misuse: Counting click-through completion as competence; ignoring overdue assignments.
metrics.protocol: Separate mandatory/compliance completion from development completion.

metrics.name: Critical-skill coverage
metrics.definition: Share of critical-skill seat demand met by employees assessed at the required proficiency.
metrics.exclusions: non-critical skills
metrics.common_misuse: Self-assessed proficiency inflating coverage; defining 'critical skill' inconsistently.
metrics.protocol: Denominator = required_critical_skill_seats (the sum of seat demand across critical skills), not a vague headcount; define the critical-skill taxonomy and the proficiency bar; avoid self-assessed proficiency inflating coverage.

metrics.name: Succession coverage (bench strength)
metrics.definition: Share of critical roles with at least one identified, ready successor.
metrics.exclusions: non-critical roles
metrics.common_misuse: Counting a named-but-not-ready successor as coverage; defining 'critical role' too broadly.
metrics.decision_supported: Key-person risk and talent-pipeline credibility (ISO 30414 succession).
metrics.protocol: Split ready-now vs ready-later (see successor_readiness); state the critical-role definition.

metrics.name: Successor readiness mix
metrics.definition: Distribution of identified successors by readiness (ready-now / 1-2 yrs / 3+ yrs).
metrics.exclusions: critical roles with no identified successor (captured by succession_coverage)
metrics.common_misuse: Reporting coverage without readiness, so a bench of all 'ready-later' looks as safe as ready-now.
metrics.decision_supported: Depth and timing of the succession bench.
metrics.protocol: Companion to succession_coverage; readiness bands defined consistently with the talent review.

metrics.name: Representation by level
metrics.definition: Demographic-group share of the population at each level.
metrics.exclusions: groups below the minimum reportable cell size (suppressed)
metrics.common_misuse: Reading a single org-wide percentage without the by-level view that shows where representation narrows.
metrics.decision_supported: DEI strategy, board reporting, regulatory context.
metrics.protocol: Always cut by level; enforce minimum cell sizes; never expose individuals.

metrics.name: Leadership diversity
metrics.definition: Demographic-group share among people-leaders / senior levels.
metrics.exclusions: below-threshold cells (suppressed)
metrics.common_misuse: Citing overall diversity while leadership lags; small-N volatility at senior levels.
metrics.protocol: Define the leadership scope (people-managers vs senior band); minimum cell sizes; pair with promotion_rate equity cuts.

metrics.name: Adverse-impact ratio (4/5ths)
metrics.definition: Selection rate of a group relative to the highest-selected reference group (EEOC four-fifths rule).
metrics.exclusions: stages/decisions with no structured selection step; below-threshold cells
metrics.common_misuse: Treating < 0.80 as automatic proof of discrimination (it is a screening flag, not a verdict); ignoring it where automated screening is used.
metrics.decision_supported: Selection-fairness monitoring, especially with automated screening.
metrics.protocol: Flag when the ratio < 0.80; minimum cell sizes; this is a flag for investigation, not a legal conclusion.

metrics.name: Revenue per FTE
metrics.definition: Trailing-twelve-month revenue divided by current full-time-equivalent headcount.
metrics.exclusions: Contractors excluded from FTE; non-recurring revenue noted separately.
metrics.common_misuse: Comparing across companies of different stage/GTM; reading one quarter instead of the trend; using headcount instead of FTE.
metrics.decision_supported: Workforce-efficiency and headcount-investment decisions (the People<->Finance bridge).
metrics.protocol: Trailing-12-month revenue over current FTE; benchmark only against similar stage/GTM and the company's own multi-year trend (RPE is a lagging indicator).

metrics.name: Operating leverage (revenue/FTE growth)
metrics.definition: Year-over-year change in revenue per FTE - whether each FTE produces more revenue over time.
metrics.exclusions: One-off revenue; M&A-driven headcount step-changes flagged.
metrics.common_misuse: Reading leverage in isolation from gross margin, churn, and CAC; attributing a revenue-driven gain to headcount cuts.
metrics.decision_supported: Whether the company is scaling efficiently (more output with proportionally fewer people).
metrics.protocol: Report alongside the revenue/FTE and headcount trend; pair with gross margin and retention so a high reading with thin margins isn't misread as health.

metrics.name: Workforce cost ratio (base-salary basis)
metrics.definition: Active-employee base salary as a share of trailing-twelve-month revenue.
metrics.exclusions: Variable pay, employer taxes, and benefits are NOT included (base-salary basis); see labor_cost_per_fte for the fully-loaded measure.
metrics.common_misuse: Presenting base-salary-only cost as fully-loaded workforce cost; comparing across companies with different equity/variable mixes.
metrics.decision_supported: Workforce-cost-to-revenue governance (the largest SaaS P&L line).
metrics.protocol: State the basis explicitly (base salary only); the fully-loaded ratio is labor_cost_per_fte and stays data_pending until benefits/taxes/variable are modeled.

metrics.name: 9-box talent grid
metrics.definition: Distribution of the rated active population across the performance x potential 9-box grid.
metrics.formula: count(active rated employees) by (performance_band x potential_band)
metrics.exclusions: Unrated employees; employees without a potential rating; contractors.
metrics.common_misuse: Treating box placement as a stack-ranking or a pay/termination trigger; comparing boxes across managers without calibration; reading the grid as fixed rather than a development snapshot.
metrics.decision_supported: Succession, development, and calibration discussions (never pay or termination).
metrics.protocol: Map the rating scale to three performance bands and pair with the potential rating; use only for development/succession conversations, never as an automated action trigger.
